title: Webex Room System Uptime
spl:
index=webex sourcetype="webex:device"
| eval up=if(connection_state="connected",1,0)
| bin _time span=1h
| stats avg(up) as uptime_ratio by _time, device_id
| where uptime_ratio < 0.99
| table _time, device_id, uptime_ratio
description: Room system availability underpins executive and boardroom SLAs; uptime trending supports hardware refresh and network path decisions.
value: Room system availability underpins executive and boardroom SLAs; uptime trending supports hardware refresh and network path decisions.
grandmaExplanation: Room system availability underpins executive and boardroom service promises; uptime trending supports hardware refresh and network path decisions — so phishing, data leaks, and account misuse do not slip through.
implementation: Derive online state from heartbeat or Control Hub connectivity. Compute rolling uptime per room vs. expected business hours. Alert on devices below 99% weekly uptime or prolonged offline spans.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `ta_cisco_webex_add_on_for_splunk`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=webex:device`, device online/offline events.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Derive online state from heartbeat or Control Hub connectivity. Compute rolling uptime per room vs. expected business hours. Alert on devices below 99% weekly uptime or prolonged offline spans.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=webex sourcetype="webex:device"
| eval up=if(connection_state="connected",1,0)
| bin _time span=1h
| stats avg(up) as uptime_ratio by _time, device_id
| where uptime_ratio < 0.99
| table _time, device_id, uptime_ratio
```

Understanding this SPL

**Webex Room System Uptime** — Room system availability underpins executive and boardroom SLAs; uptime trending supports hardware refresh and network path decisions.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=webex:device`, device online/offline events. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `ta_cisco_webex_add_on_for_splunk`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: webex; **sourcetype**: webex:device. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=webex, sourcetype="webex:device". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **up** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by _time, device_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Filters the current rows with `where uptime_ratio < 0.99` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Webex Room System Uptime**): table _time, device_id, uptime_ratio


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (uptime ratio by room), Single value (fleet uptime %), Table (rooms below SLA).
visualization: Line chart (uptime ratio by room), Single value (fleet uptime %), Table (rooms below SLA).
